summary: 8 concrete tools and services surfaced from HN comment threads.

items.name: Openrsync
items.source_story_title: Openrsync: An implementation of rsync, by the OpenBSD team
items.summary: Openrsync is an OpenBSD rsync implementation with working exclude handling.
items.why_included: Commenters said it works great and confirmed --exclude works on OpenBSD 7.9.
items.evidence: HN comments said openrsync works great, and one commenter confirmed --exclude now works.

items.name: DuckDB
items.source_story_title: SQLite is all you need for durable workflows
items.summary: DuckDB is an in-process analytics database commenters said they use and like.
items.why_included: A direct positive mention in a thread about durable workflow storage choices.
items.evidence: One commenter said, “I use DuckDB and like it.”

items.name: Postgres
items.source_story_title: SQLite is all you need for durable workflows
items.summary: Postgres is the SQL database commenters still trust for durable workflows at scale.
items.why_included: Multiple comments framed Postgres as the practical alternative for networked workloads.
items.evidence: Commenters said “Personally I like Postgres for this reason too” and that it works fine at decent scale.

items.name: Cline
items.summary: Cline is an open-source coding agent IDE extension that commenters say works well.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it as a low-friction coding tool.
items.evidence: One HN commenter wrote that Cline “works well enough and doesn’t require any sign-up.”

items.name: TrustedRouter
items.summary: TrustedRouter is an encrypted OpenRouter alternative with hosted and self-hosted options.
items.why_included: A concrete new product name and URL surfaced in the same OpenRouter discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter said they were offering “a fully end to end encrypted open source version and hosted version of open router.”

items.name: Cloudflare Speed Test
items.summary: Cloudflare Speed Test is a browser-based internet speed checker from Cloudflare.
items.why_included: It got an explicit fan endorsement as an Ookla alternative.
items.evidence: A commenter said they’re “a huge fan of https://speed.cloudflare.com/.”

items.name: SpeedOf.Me
items.summary: SpeedOf.Me is a browser-based internet speed test commenters recommended.
items.why_included: A simple direct recommendation in the same speed-test thread.
items.evidence: One commenter answered with a plain “use https://speedof.me”.

items.name: OpenSpeedTest
items.summary: OpenSpeedTest is a free HTML5 speed test positioned as an easy alternative.
items.why_included: It was suggested as a direct way to bypass the usual speed-test business model.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote that “all it takes to defeat the business model is https://openspeedtest.com/.”
